Name: Calém
Address: Av. Diogo Leite 344, 4400-111 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Sensory barrel museum, optional Fado show combo. Best for first-timers. Combo: cellar + Live Fado + tasting €28

Name: Graham's
Address: Rua do Agro 141, 4400-003 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Hilltop panoramic views, leather-chaired Vintage Room (€60–70). Most thorough wine education. Premium + cheese/chocolate pairing €40

Name: Poças
Address: Rua Visconde das Devesas 224, 4401-337 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Rare Portuguese family-owned lodge (since 1918). Intimate and personal. Aged Tawny Colheitas are the specialty

Name: Cockburn's
Address: Rua Serpa Pinto 346, 4400-307 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Largest wooden Port cellar in Gaia. Only lodge with a working cooperage — watch coopers repair barrels by hand. John Smith's Room premium tasting €50–75

Name: Quevedo
Address: Rua de Santa Marinha 77, 4400-291 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Young family-run producer (1993) in a 200-year-old cooperage. Communal tables, wine bar + cocktails. Port + 3 cheeses pairing €26

Name: Taylor's
Address: Rua do Choupelo 250, 4400-088 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: One of oldest Port houses (1692). Hilltop gardens, peacock, self-guided audio tour (13 languages). Best scenic spot in Gaia

Name: Burmester
Address: Largo Ponte Luiz I, 4400-111 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Most conveniently located — right at foot of Dom Luís I Bridge. Small, less crowded. Aged Tawnies are the house specialty. 3 aged Ports + cheese + chocolate €45

Name: Ferreira
Address: Av. Ramos Pinto 70, 4400-082 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Founded 1751, built into an old convent. Most culturally Portuguese lodge. Legacy of Dona Antónia Adelaide Ferreira. Book via ferreira.visitors@aaferreira.pt

Name: Quinta do Noval
Address: Av. de Diogo Leite 256, 4400-111 Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto
Tips: Walk-in wine shop/bar — no cellar tour. Great Douro views. Vintage + Ruby + 20-Year and 40-Year Tawny flights. No booking needed

Name: Café Santiago
Tips: Porto's most iconic francesinha institution (est. 1959). Proprietary family sauce since 1959. Arrive before noon to avoid queues

Name: Yuko Tavern
Tips: Evening-only, reservations needed. Oven-baked francesinha. Best sangria in Porto. Half-portion available

Name: Brasão Cervejaria
Address: Aliados: Rua Ramalho Ortigão 28 / Coliseu: Rua Passos Manuel 205, Porto
Tips: Polished rustic-chic beer house. Craft beers, vegetarian options. Good for mixed groups. Same group as Yuko

Name: Lado B Café
Tips: Music-themed cervejaaria opposite the Coliseu. Famous chocolate cake (reportedly 'best in the world')

Name: Tappas Caffé
Address: Rua António Granjo 549, Candal, 4440-452 Vila Nova de Gaia
Tips: Rare wood-burning oven francesinha — genuine grilled cheese. Spicy 'men's sauce' option. Free xiripiti shot included

Name: Santa Francesinha
Tips: 8 types including Porto's best vegan francesinha (seitan + soy chorizo + vegan sauce). Short wait likely

Name: Cufra
Tips: Est. 1974 — one of Porto's oldest. Sauce descended from Regaleira (the very first francesinha restaurant). Only 3 people know the recipe

Name: Venham Mais 5
Tips: Originally Porto's top prego destination. Francesinha earns equal acclaim. Exceptional hand-cut fries. Chocolate mountain dessert

Name: Capa Negra II
Tips: Porto's most beloved brewery, perpetually buzzing with locals. Famous city-wide for rissóis (fried dumplings). Soft-style sauce

Name: A Cantarinha Café
Address: Largo da Maternidade de Júlio Dinis 75, 4050 Porto
Tips: Family-run neighbourhood café. Owner refills your table with house-fried chips — whether you ask or not. Warm and unfussy

Name: Francesinha Café
Tips: Specialist francesinha-only diner near Marquês metro. Outstanding quality, rotating surprise topping each visit. Small and always full

Name: Casa de Pasto O Golfinho
Address: Rua de Sá de Noronha 137, 4050-526 Porto
Tips: Tiny cash-only hole-in-the-wall. Sparkling clean, wildly popular with locals. One of Porto's most authentic experiences. Cash only

Name: Madureira's
Tips: Est. 1969. Multi-location seafood-first group where the francesinha holds its own. Kitchen dials back heat — smooth, non-spicy sauce

Name: Escama
Address: Rua de Mouzinho da Silveira 203, 4050-421 Porto
Tips: Modern sophisticated contemporary seafood. Creative Atlantic cuisine. Turbot with hazelnut vinaigrette is the standout. Book 1 week ahead

Name: Culto ao Bacalhau
Address: Mercado do Bolhão, Rua Formosa 322, 4000-248 Porto
Tips: Michelin-recommended codfish specialist inside Bolhão Market. 20+ cod preparations. Roasted cod with corn bread crust is the standout. Book ahead

Name: Taberna dos Mercadores
Tips: Tiny 16-seat gem in Ribeira. Weeks-in-advance reservations essential. Best octopus rice in Porto. Email ahead

Name: Terra Nova
Tips: Polished brasserie-style on Ribeira waterfront. Large Douro-view windows. Good refined lunch or dinner with river views. Walk-in

Name: Ostras & Coisas
Tips: Long-standing reference for fresh oysters and shellfish. Outstanding sparkling wine list. Fresh oysters + Mix shellfish platter

Name: Meia Nau Porto
Tips: City-centre branch of beloved Matosinhos institution. Nautical-themed, fast service. Traditional fish & bread stew (galo frito com açorda de ovas)

Name: Bacalhau
Tips: Small intimate restaurant on ancient Ribeira river wall. Most authentic atmosphere in the area. Creative codfish in all forms

Name: Terreiro
Tips: Small popular restaurant with fantastic Douro views. Generous food at fair prices. Arrive at opening or book

Name: Marisqueira do Porto
Tips: Relaxed neighbourhood marisqueira in Campo Alegre. Fair prices. Also serves francesinha — good for mixed groups

Name: O Gaveto ⭐
Tips: The quintessential answer to 'best seafood in Porto.' Matosinhos institution since 1984. Krug Ambassade distinction 2025. The splurge. Order from the display

Name: Restaurante Lusíadas
Tips: Premium three-generation family restaurant. Michelin-recommended, sommelier, curated wine list. Stuffed crab and arroz de marisco are outstanding. Book ahead

Name: Salta o Muro
Tips: No-frills Matosinhos staple — plastic chairs, sharing tables. True local experience. Octopus rice on Fridays; seasonal grilled sardines

Name: Mercado – Food & Drinks
Tips: Unique concept: pick your fish at market stalls, fishmongers prep it, restaurant cooks it. Notably reasonable vs the Rua Heróis strip

Name: Jácome
Tips: Dark no-frills restaurant past Matosinhos. Powerful smell of fish signals authenticity. Serious destination for flavour above everything else

Name: O Filipe de Angeiras
Address: Avenida Praia de Angeiras 160, 4455-188 Lavra (near Matosinhos)
Tips: One of very few restaurants with a terrace directly facing the Atlantic. Enormous starters list, festive vibe. Order Muros Antigos vinho verde

Name: Confeitaria do Bolhão
Tips: Porto's oldest pastry shop (est. 1896). Beautiful Art Nouveau interior opposite Bolhão market. Opens 6am — great for departure day

Name: Leitaria da Quinta do Paço
Tips: Historic dairy-turned-pastry shop (1920s). Famous for artisanal whipped-cream éclairs in 8 fixed flavours + seasonal varieties. Breakfast menus from €3.90

Name: Molete Bread & Breakfast
Tips: Local bakery mini-chain. Budget-friendly breakfast menus from €1. Bôla saloia (traditional savoury Portuguese cake) is the standout

Name: Confeitaria Chicana
Address: Avenida de Rodrigues de Freitas 36, 4300-096 Porto
Tips: Family-run pastry shop in Bonfim. Won Sábado magazine's Best Croissant in Porto. Entirely local crowd. Award-winning croissants + húngaros biscuits

Name: SO Coffee Roasters
Address: Rua Sá de Noronha 119, Porto (Aliados area)
Tips: Small bright specialty coffee bar. Choose your brew method (Chemex, V60, AeroPress) and single-origin bean. For coffee geeks. Largely unknown even to locals

Name: DaTerra Baixa
Address: Rua de Mouzinho da Silveira 249, 4050-421 Porto
Tips: Porto's go-to vegan breakfast. Full buffet Mon–Fri 08:30–11:30. Vegan pastéis de nata, açaí bowls, detox juices. Modern stylish setting near São Bento

Name: Época Porto
Tips: Tiny Nordic-inspired café. Seasonal organic ingredients. Always a queue — arrive early. Sourdough toast; organic seasonal plates. Worth the wait

Name: Hakko
Tips: Seasonal breakfast/brunch café by a former Época owner. La Cabra (Copenhagen) specialty coffee. Brilliant croissants ('to die for'), Turkish eggs, French toast. Arrive early

Name: BICHO Porto
Tips: Sun-drenched plant-filled café with a full glass wall. Rotating monthly French toast (rabanada) — new flavour every month. Social mixing point for locals and expats

Name: Early (Cedofeita)
Tips: Sustainability-focused breakfast/brunch café in artsy Cedofeita. Seasonal, locally sourced, house-baked artisan bread. Outstanding food; atmosphere slightly snobbish

Name: A Certain Café
Address: Rua do Morgado de Mateus 107, 4000-333 Porto
Tips: Drip-coffee-only specialty café — no espresso menu. Vegetable-forward menu, sourdough toast. Perfect for slow unhurried breakfast away from tourist crowds

Name: Royale Pão Paixão
Tips: Modern artisan bakery-café in Boavista. Sourdough, pastries, bright dining room. Heavy on sweet options. Lunch Mon–Fri, brunch Sat. Banana bread, pastéis de nata

Name: A Bolacheira
Tips: Porto's only café specialising exclusively in cookies and cupcakes. Quiet Foz street. Everything homemade fresh daily, rotating creative flavours. Very affordable

Name: Pão – Padaria Artesanal
Address: Rua de Diu 365 (Mercado da Foz, Foz do Douro)
Tips: Artisan bakery stall inside Foz market near the ocean. Organic flours, slow fermentation. Savoury toast with Portuguese canned fish is unique. Sourdough with local roaster coffee

Name: Manteigaria ⭐
Tips: Reddit's unanimous #1 for standalone pastéis de nata in Porto. Standing counter only — grab a nata straight from the oven, sprinkle cinnamon, eat immediately. Pair with bica

Name: Fábrica da Nata
Address: Rua de Santa Catarina 225, Porto (multiple locations)
Tips: Reliable less touristy nata café. Consistent quality without hype or queues of Manteigaria. Multiple city-centre locations

Name: Aloma 🏆
Tips: Multi-award-winning nata — Best Pastel de Nata 2024–2025. Richer, more egg-forward custard than competitors. Airport location perfect on departure day

Name: Tabuleta
Tips: Classic Portuguese tasca — honest, generous, no shortcuts. The kind of neighbourhood restaurant locals have been eating at for decades. Walk-in; arrive by 7pm

Name: MUU
Tips: Contemporary Portuguese with strong focus on quality beef and seasonal local produce. Sleek interior, serious kitchen. Reservations essential — book 1–2 weeks ahead

Name: Eleit
Tips: Relaxed modern local cuisine. Good value for the quality. Popular with young Porto professionals. Recommended to book

Name: Taberna de São Miguel
Tips: Traditional petiscos bar. Try tripas à moda do Porto — the dish that earned locals the nickname tripeiros. Walk-in or call ahead

Name: Restaurante O Rafael
Tips: Beloved local institution steps from Matosinhos beach. No-frills interior but grilled fish is exceptional — fishermen's catch bought fresh that morning. Walk-in; arrive before noon or 7pm

Name: Loca
Tips: Porto's most talked-about creative seafood of 2025–26. Small, exclusive, chef-driven. Menu changes with the catch and season. Book 2–3 weeks ahead — essential in August

Name: Cobre
Address: Vila Nova de Gaia (river side, near Dom Luís I Bridge)
Tips: Contemporary Portuguese in Gaia with terrace offering bridge views over Porto. High-quality ingredients, excellent wine list. Ideal for a special last-night dinner. Book in advance

Name: Casa da Bifana
Address: Near Rua de Passos Manuel, Porto
Tips: Porto's iconic bifana stop — pork cutlet in a crusty roll with garlicky wine sauce. The definitive cheap fast Porto street food. Open late. Cash preferred. Eat immediately while hot

Name: Picha Café
Tips: Young locals' favourite bar. Good cocktails at reasonable prices, relaxed atmosphere, standing room inside and out. Where Porto twenty-somethings start their evenings

Name: Base
Tips: Cocktail bar near São Bento. Tighter menu than Galeria de Paris bars but consistently well-executed drinks. Good music, compact space. Craft cocktails; seasonal specials

Name: Bar Clássico
Address: Near Rua dos Poveiros area, Porto
Tips: Old-school Portuguese bar unchanged in 30 years. Cheap, authentic, utterly unpretentious. Mostly local regulars. House wine €1.50/glass. Dirt cheap and utterly authentic

Name: The Yeatman (terrace bar)
Address: Rua do Choupelo 88, 4400-088 Vila Nova de Gaia
Tips: Luxury wine hotel in Gaia with the most spectacular panoramic view of Porto. Open to non-guests. Wine flights (€15–25/person): curated Port + Douro Valley. Arrive before sunset

Name: Gelataria Portuense
Address: Rua de Santa Catarina area (central Porto)
Tips: Classic homemade gelato, natural flavours, fresh Portuguese milk. Top picks: chocolate hazelnut, salted caramel. Small, no-frills, very local feel. One of Porto's most consistent

Name: NIVÀ Gelato
Address: Several locations: Time Out Market, Matosinhos, and more
Tips: Italian roots (founded in the 80s), rich and smooth. Dark chocolate ganache, stracciatella, ricotta with fig. The ricotta & fig flavour is extraordinary

Name: Neveiros
Tips: Porto institution — not just gelato but the city's most creative ice cream parlour. Pistachio affogato (pistachio gelato + hot espresso shot) is the star. Perfect after a morning of churches

Name: Santini
Address: Rua de Santa Catarina / Foz, Porto
Tips: Iconic Lisbon gelateria now in Porto. Ultra-fresh natural flavours. The fresh strawberry (seasonal) and lemon are world-class when in season. Foz location has outdoor terrace

Name: Cremosi
Tips: Local favourite, very consistent, silky smooth texture, playful flavours. One of the most reliable day-to-day choices in Porto

Name: DONKO Gelato
Tips: Opened December 2024 — artisan gelato by a Portuguese-Argentine couple. Very smooth, strong natural flavours of local ingredients. Worth seeking out

Name: Cremita
Tips: Handmade ice cream with some of Porto's most creative flavours. Seasonal specials. Cedofeita neighbourhood location is lovely for walking and exploring
